# Test & benchmark fixtures

Shared image fixtures, deliberately spanning the input types codecs behave
differently on. Used by the e2e suite (`tests/e2e/`) and the benchmark
(`benchmarks/`).

| File | What | Source |
|------|------|--------|
| `photo.jpg` | Real photograph, 1024×683 (lots of colour / high entropy) | downloaded from picsum.photos (seed `sqush-photo`) |
| `photo-large.jpg` | Big photograph, 4000×3000 (12 MP) — stress / "do big images differ?" | downloaded from picsum.photos (seed `sqush-large`) |
| `illustration.png` | Flat shapes, ~6 solid colours, sharp edges (lossless-friendly; exposes JPEG ringing) | generated by `generate.mjs` |
| `transparent.png` | RGBA with a full 0→255 alpha range (opaque disc + alpha-gradient band) — alpha tests | generated by `generate.mjs` |

## Regenerating the synthetic fixtures

```sh
node tests/fixtures/generate.mjs
```

Deterministic (pure math, no deps) — produces identical `illustration.png` and
`transparent.png` every time. The photographic fixtures are real downloaded
photos (synthetic noise isn't representative of real photo entropy); the picsum
seeds are fixed so they're reproducible too.

## Notes

- Why multiple types: a codec change can improve photos but hurt flat
  illustrations, break alpha, or behave differently on huge inputs. The fixtures
  cover all four so a regression in any of them is caught.
- Swap `photo.jpg` / `photo-large.jpg` for your own images for article-quality
  numbers; if you do, re-capture the benchmark baseline.
